title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Recovery · Runtime Contract: Delete Marker Resolution
summary: A team enables versioning after an incident, assumes delete markers are backups, and applies compliance retention without testing irreversible governance and restore procedures. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the delete marker resolution increment in version and immutable-retention recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: List versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Delete Marker Resolution · Theory Delete Marker Resolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 1829 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: List versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Delete Marker Resolution contract for the supplied data.
